sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचपरं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् । पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ॥
hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले  परम ब्रह्म, परम धाम और महान् पवित्र आप ही हैं ।  आप शाश्वत, दिव्य पुरुष, आदिदेव, अजन्मा और विभु व्यापक हैं  ऐसा सबकेसब ऋषि, देवर्षि नारद, असित, देवल तथा व्यास कहते हैं और स्वयं आप भी मेरे प्रति कहते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna said: You are the supreme reality, the highest refuge, the purest one, the eternal divine person, the first being, unborn, and all-pervading.
essence: Recognition turns into surrender when the highest reality stands revealed.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna recognises Krishna as the highest reality, the purest refuge, eternal, unborn, and present everywhere.
simpleInsight: Awe can become clarity: the mind bows when it sees what is truly greater than itself.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna has just been overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic revelation. In that stunned silence, he names Krishna as the highest reality, the pure refuge, and the eternal source of all beings.
modernRelevance: You stand before someone’s brutal honesty and feel your defenses collapse. In that moment, respect becomes recognition: what is larger than your fear deserves your surrender.
translationLiteral: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / परं (supreme) / ब्रह्म (supreme reality) / परं (highest) / धाम (abode/refuge) / पवित्रं (pure) / परमं (highest) / भवान् (you are) / पुरुषं (person) / शाश्वतं (eternal) / दिव्यम् (divine) / आदिदेवम् (first being) / अजम् (unborn) / विभुम् (all-pervading)
insight: It shifts the mind from seeing Krishna as a teacher to seeing him as the source behind everything.
